campaign: Code Runs, Disputes Don't. Enter Internet Court

selected.content:
Your DAO treasury was drained. The code was correct. What now?

DAOs, DeFi protocols, and AI agents operate across jurisdictions. No single court has authority.

Internet Court (internetcourt.org) is the missing layer.

Statements must be specific and evaluable.
Evidence has defined formats.
AI validators reach independent consensus.

No judges. No borders. No months of waiting.

We're entering an era where code executes billions in value automatically.

But execution without recourse is just half the system.

The other half is finally here.

What's the last word in your smart contract? Who decides what it means?

allVersions.content:
Your DAO treasury was drained. The code was correct. What now?

DAOs, DeFi protocols, and AI agents operate across jurisdictions. No single court has authority.

Internet Court (internetcourt.org) is the missing layer.

Statements must be specific and evaluable.
Evidence has defined formats.
AI validators reach independent consensus.

No judges. No borders. No months of waiting.

We're entering an era where code executes billions in value automatically.

But execution without recourse is just half the system.

The other half is finally here.

What's the last word in your smart contract? Who decides what it means?

$50 million left The DAO in 2016. A bug. No recourse.

When your agent trades with my agent and something goes wrong, neither of us can show up in court.

This is the structural gap.

Internet Court (internetcourt.org) proposes a framework built for the internet.

AI jury evaluates on-chain and off-chain evidence.
Verdict: TRUE, FALSE, or UNDETERMINED.

Resolutions in minutes, for pennies.

The future of commerce is autonomous.

The future of dispute resolution has to match.

Code runs. Now disputes can too.

Who resolves disputes in your DAO? Have you thought about it?

AI agents negotiate instantly. Human courts move at human speed.

Traditional courts require physical presence, real identities, and months of proceedings. The internet economy has none of these.

This is what Internet Court (internetcourt.org) solves.

The missing enforcement layer for the agent economy.
AI-powered. Borderless. Fast.

The internet finally has its own court.

AI agents will soon execute more agreements than all human lawyers combined.

They need their own court.

It exists now. The question is: are we ready to use it?

How does your project handle cross-border, pseudonymous arbitration?

Your AI assistant just made a legally binding agreement. With another AI.

The average court case takes 18 months. In crypto, that's 3 bull markets and 5 protocol upgrades.

Internet Court (internetcourt.org) provides dispute resolution at machine speed.

AI validators evaluate evidence. Minutes, not months.

True, False, or Undetermined. Clear verdicts.

The agent economy is scaling. AI-to-AI transactions are growing exponentially.

When agents disagree, they need their own resolution mechanism.

Not built for humans. Built for the internet.

When the agent economy arrives, will you be ready for the disputes?

The transaction confirmed. The other party claims fraud. Who decides?

A cross-border smart contract dispute can cost more in legal fees than the disputed amount. The system is broken for small claims.

Enter Internet Court (internetcourt.org).

Clear statements. Defined evidence constraints. AI jury consensus.

Verdicts delivered in minutes, globally, pseudonymously.

More DAOs. More DeFi. More autonomous agreements. More inevitable disputes.

The question isn't whether we need this infrastructure.

It's whether we build it before we need it.

If your DAO treasury was drained tomorrow, where would you seek recourse?
